rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively Motion For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, Mar-11-2026, Line 1. PETITIONER PROGRESSIVE INSURANCE'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT , Or Alternatively Motion For Summary Adjudication. Petitioner Progressive Insurance's motion for summary judgment or alternatively, summary adjudication is denied. ***THE COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: JASON DEWITT VS. JACINTO JOSE VALDOVINO ET AL **Provide Access**
rulings.Calendar Matter: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, Mar-11-2026, Line 4. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. Pursuant to the court's order to show cause, Jacinto Jose Valdovino's demurrer to Jason Dewitt's answer to Valdovino's cross-complaint is ordered off calendar as mooted by Dewitt's amended answer, filed on December 8, 2025. The court vacates its order of December 12, 2025 granting Dewitt leave to amend; that order was mooted by the filing of the December 8 amended answer. Valdovino seeks further relief, asking the court to extend his time to demur or otherwise respond to the amended answer because he did not receive service of the amended answer. Any relief not contemplated in the court's order to show cause must be sought by separate motion. The court notes, however, that the December 8 amended answer appears to attach a proper proof of service. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Defendant Gulshan Ghori'S Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admission And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One By Plaintiff Cesar Augusto Portela Barreto; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, Mar-11-2026, Line 6. 1-DEFENDANT GULSHAN GOHRI'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Compel Discovery Responses To Defendant Gulshan Ghori'S Special Interrogatories, Form Interrogatories, Requests For Admission And Request For Production Of Documents, Set One By Plaintiff Cesar Augusto Portela Barreto. The unopposed motion to compel plaintiff Cesar Augusto Portela Barreto to respond to defendant Gulshan Ghori's Form Interrogatories, Set One, Special Interrogatories, Set One, Request for Production of Documents, Set One, and Requests for Admission, Set One, is granted in part. The remedy for failing to respond to RFAs is a deemed-admitted order. The motion is otherwise granted. (See Code Civ. Proc., secs. 2030.290, subd. (c), 2031.300, subd. (c).) Plaintiff shall provide complete, objection-free responses within 20 days of notice of entry of this order. Defendant does not seek sanctions. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendant Gulshan Rai Gohri To Have Requests For Admission To Plaintiff, Set One, Be Deemed Admitted; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, Mar-11-2026, Line 6. 2-DEFENDANT GULSHAN GOHRI'S, AN INDIVIDUAL, Motion To Have Requests For Admission To Plaintiff, Set One, Be Deemed Admitted. The unopposed motion for a deemed-admitted order as to defendant Gulshan Ghori's first set of requests for admissions to plaintiff Cesar Augusto Portela Barreto is granted. Plaintiff failed to timely respond to the requests and has not responded to them. (Code Civ. Proc., sec. 2033.280.) Sanctions are mandatory in this circumstance. (Id., sec. 2033.280, subd. (c).) The court orders plaintiff to pay $100 to defendant within 30 days of notice of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: ARNOLD A RAMOS ET AL VS. HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery / Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, Mar-11-2026, Line 8. 1-DEFENDANT HYUNDAI MOTOR AMERICA'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST Amended COMPLAINT. Defendant Hyundai Motor America's motion to strike the First Amended Complaint's punitive damages prayer is denied. By companion order today, the court has overruled the demurrer to plaintiffs Arnold Ramos et al.'s fraudulent concealment cause of action. A fraudulent concealment cause of action may give rise to punitive damages. Here, Ramos alleges that the fraudulent concealment exposed him to serious safety hazards, including the including the risk of overheating and spontaneous combustion in operating the car. (FAC, para. 50.) The court concludes that plaintiffs have adequately alleged facts giving rise to a claim for punitive damages. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
